cost_components_summary: Annualised hot-water equipment and service-delivery cost excluding explicit commodity purchases and carbon costs.
input_basis_notes: Direct final-energy inputs only. Electricity-related emissions are excluded from end-use coefficients and represented by electricity supply methods.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct on-site emissions only. Electricity-related emissions are excluded from end-use coefficients to avoid double counting.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative stock-turnover envelope only; no explicit household stock model in this aggregate pathway representation.
availability_conditions: Available in all milestone years as an aggregate hot-water service pathway.
evidence_summary: Incumbent hot-water fuel mix is split from aggregate residential building service evidence for the comparison pilot.
derivation_method: V1 reduced-form allocation using a fixed water-heating activity coefficient and incumbent final-energy shares.
review_notes: Declining max_share is an indicative availability envelope for retaining incumbent mixed-fuel hot-water service.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Can expand into gas storage/instantaneous, resistive electric, electric heat-pump, and solar/boosted water-heating technologies.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Maps to a useful hot-water demand service with competing water-heating technologies in a TIMES-style demand module.

cost_components_summary: Annualised appliance and installation/service cost excluding explicit commodity purchases and carbon costs.
input_basis_notes: Direct final-energy inputs per useful hot-water service unit. Electricity-related emissions are excluded from end-use coefficients and represented by electricity supply methods.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct on-site emissions only. Electricity-related emissions are excluded from end-use coefficients to avoid double counting.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative appliance-stock availability envelope only; no explicit stock rollover model in this representation.
availability_conditions: Available in all milestone years when the technology-bundle representation is selected.
evidence_summary: Representative gas water-heater technology with high direct combustion input and low auxiliary electricity use.
derivation_method: Exploratory gas technology row using fixed final-energy intensity and direct combustion factor.
review_notes: Declining max_share is an indicative envelope for fossil appliance replacement.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Can expand into stock vintages, tank sizes, building archetypes, boost configurations, and explicit appliance capacity.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Maps to a useful hot-water demand with competing appliance technologies in a TIMES-style demand module.
